name: openapi-typescript (OpenAPI → TypeScript Types)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Drew Powers and contributors

auth.notes: Local code generation tool; no auth

performance.notes: Code generation at build time; zero runtime overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Generates TypeScript types from OpenAPI 3.x specs. `npx openapi-typescript schema.yaml -o types.ts`. No runtime code — pure type generation. Companion openapi-fetch uses generated types for fully-typed fetch calls: `client.GET('/users/{id}', {params: {path: {id: 1}}})`. Zero-runtime overhead. Path params, query params, request bodies, responses all typed. OpenAPI 3.1 support. Watch mode for development. Alternative: Orval generates full SDK (more code, more opinions). swagger-typescript-api also generates code.

gotchas: Type-only — no runtime validation (pair with Zod for that)
OpenAPI spec must be well-formed for good output
v7 has breaking changes from v6 (new types format)
Complex allOf/oneOf schemas may generate verbose types
best_when: TypeScript projects consuming REST APIs with OpenAPI specs
Generating types from backend OpenAPI specs for frontend safety
Avoiding hand-written type maintenance for API contracts
CI pipelines where API types must stay in sync with spec
avoid_when: No OpenAPI spec available (use manual types or Zod)
Need runtime validation not just types (add Zod on top)
summary: openapi-typescript generates zero-runtime TypeScript types from OpenAPI 3.x specs. `npx openapi-typescript schema.yaml -o types.ts`. Companion openapi-fetch provides fully-typed fetch calls. Pure type generation — no runtime overhead. Path params, query params, request bodies all typed. Best for TypeScript projects consuming REST APIs with OpenAPI specs to keep types in sync with API contracts.
